entry.title: Re: Modify mail text in work item
entry.content:
Hi. 

Sorry for the delay in answering. 

I have been able to fill the text in the task. Now my problem is: the mail is been sent four or five days after we created the purchasing order but we don't know how this mail is sent. 

Do you know anywhere in customizing or in the workflow where we could custom an automatic mail?

Thanks a lot.

Marta.

entry.title: Re: Fundamentals of Security in SAP BTP
entry.content: It is very comprehensive, I would like to know if it is possible to create a Client Credential for a technical service to manage sub-accounts under the given global account.

entry.title: Re: PaPm cloud Count number employees or other field
entry.content:
Hi @juancortes,

I would suggest you to use a window aggregate function to count the employee numbers in a (model) calculation (in universal model). According to your requirement following window function might be used:

COUNT(DISTINCT EMPLOYEE) OVER (PARTITION BY AREA) 

COUNT(DISTINCT EMPLOYEE) OVER (PARTITION BY YEAR-MONTH)

I hope this would be helpful.

Best regards,

Zhiyi

entry.title: Re: How do we create a variable on WEBI that calculates i...
entry.content: the formula "Couple" is not available in webi, could your please provide another formula

entry.title: Re: Identity and Access Management with Microsoft Entra, Part I: Managing access to SAP BTP
entry.content:
@MartinRaepple Great guide, and looking forward for the upcoming ones. Especially looking for the "deeper" integration which also populates SAP Cloud Identity's identity directory, in line with our strategic direction (see CIO guide).

A detail: I see you only add OIDC scope "offline_access" to the trust between IAS and Entra. I suggest adding the standard OIDC scopes "profile" and "email", to make sure BTP gets the typical user attributes in the standard token claims.

entry.title: Unable to populate data in sales order AVC using power automate & scripting
entry.content:
Dear Team,

I stuck at point where I am unable to automate the sales order creation process using MS power automate.

The Sales order item is AVC configurable.

The nitial GUI Screen data entry done well. When I go for AVC Screen data entry It dont work. 

Power Automate Error - Failed to write in textbox (Textbox wasn't found)

Text to fill in - %CurrentItem['CHARECTERISTIC']%

entry.title: Re: Overview of Custom Routing
entry.content:
very informative, 

Question : i am trying to route RequisitionBudgetCheckExportRequest to different system other than default one.

Default system : Ariba Test <>R3U100
Trying to route document type RequisitionBudgetCheckExportRequest to R3S100 from Ariba Test and tried multiple time with different conditions but this document type is not routing to R3S100. Can you please suggest what i doing wrong

entry.title: Re: CDS Data Model for Analytical List Page
entry.content:
@RadekP 

For an ALP, a normal CDS View with measure fields annotated with Aggregation.default would be sufficient, and there is no need to create an analytical query.

entry.title: Multiple Ledgers in Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear All,

We will define multiple ledgers these are 0L(IFRS) and 2L(J-GAAP) according to SAP recommend in Public Cloud.
In this regard, I have two questions.

①There are no plans that My customer will use IFRS yet.
In the future, they will use IFRS(Abroad expansion).
In this case, is it not necessary that My customer process 0L about closing operation?
If there is task, let me know about that.

②
If they will use multiple local account standard (multiple countries), must they define such local account standard in 2L every conpany code?
※ At the time of introduction, they only need to activate 0L and 2L?

Best Regards,

Yuki Tsumura

entry.title: Implement Currency Conversion in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Objective

Currency Conversion is an important aspect of any data modelling, planning, and reporting. SAP Datasphere software provides a standard functionality for Currency conversion. However, this is a quick step by step process on how currency conversion can be implemented with federation using S/4 HANA as a source. This article is completely based on my learning experience. I hope, this article will be useful for those consultants who are looking to capitalize the virtualization, federation, and tailor the needs based of the custom requirement.

How is it  different from standard approach?

1. This approach does not need all the currency relevant tables for the basic currency conversion but have the minimum four tables that are primarily involved as mentioned in the pre-requisites

2. Tables are federated or remote or virtual physical present in S/4 HANA. Unless there is a need for persistency can be scheduled at the input layer

3. Tables when remotely configured can be named according the naming conventions of the business need purposes

Prerequisites

  • Connection of SAP S/4 HANA on Premise or Cloud systems
  • Below standard tables activated in S/4 HANA systems

Table Name

Table Description

TCURV

 Exchange rate types

TCURX

 Decimal places in currencies

TCURR

 Exchange rates

TCURF

 Conversion factors

Option 1:

 Code snippet for SQL view

SELECT "FISCALYEARPERIOD", "PRODUCT","COMPANYCODE", "TRANSACTIONCURRENCY","AMOUNTINTRANSACTIONCURRENCY"

 

CONVERT_CURRENCY( AMOUNT => "AMOUNTINTRANSACTIONCURRENCY",

SOURCE_UNIT => "TRANSACTIONCURRENCY",

   "CONVERSION_TYPE" => 'M',

    "REFERENCE_DATE" => '2024-09-01', ----can be an input parameter

    "CLIENT" => '400',

    "SCHEMA" => 'DW_DEV_SCHEMA',

    "ERROR_HANDLING" => 'set_to_null',

    "TARGET_UNIT" => 'SAR', -----can be an input parameter

"PRECISIONS_TABLE" => 'S4H_TCURX', --the table used for conversion must be declared here this is federated with remote table call

    "CONFIGURATION_TABLE" => 'S4H_TCURV', --the table used for conversion must be declared here this is federated with remote table call

    "PREFACTORS_TABLE" => 'S4H_TCURF', --the table used for conversion must be declared here this is federated with remote table call

    "RATES_TABLE" => 'S4H_TCURR' --the table used for conversion must be declared here this is federated with remote table call

/***These remote tables when imported have been named with prefix as S4H in this case.

)

AS deb_cre_lc,

 

from "FIGL_LINEITEM" ----ACDOCA standard SAP Transaction Table

Resultset:

When converted from USD to SAR

Option 2:

Case of calculated column in a graphical view

Calculated column dbe_cre_lc definition

Code snippet

CONVERT_CURRENCY( AMOUNT => "AMOUNTINTRANSACTIONCURRENCY",

SOURCE_UNIT => "TRANSACTIONCURRENCY",

   "CONVERSION_TYPE" => 'M',

    "REFERENCE_DATE" => :IP_REFERENCE_DATE,

    "CLIENT" => '400',

    "SCHEMA" => ‘DW_DEV_SCHEMA',

    "ERROR_HANDLING" => 'set_to_null',

    "TARGET_UNIT" => :IP_TARGET_CURRENCY,

"PRECISIONS_TABLE" => 'S4H_TCURX',---federated tables from S/4 HANA

"CONFIGURATION_TABLE" => 'S4H_TCURV', ,---federated tables from S/4 HANA

"PREFACTORS_TABLE" => 'S4H_TCURF', ,---federated tables from S/4 HANA

"RATES_TABLE" => 'S4H_TCURR') ,---federated tables from S/4 HANA

INPUT PARAMETERS

Resultset:

entry.title: half day and full day unpaid absence calculation in UK payroll
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

in SAP payroll we often get requirement to calculate salary when employee is on half-day unpaid absence. Recently I have implemented the rule for one of my client where I came across the following requirement:

According to this requirement,I changed tables :

 V_554S_G , v_t554c, V_T554L, V_T554E

But apparently ,it dosenot any effection in monthly pay Halfday absence unpaid , because in pay roll in schema :

question : can I do any customising for handel it or should I chnage schema?

entry.title: SYSTEM user locked
entry.content:
Hi,

I reset the password of system user of systemdb, but when i tried to login it fails... i see the below messages in the nameserver trace file...

Please help to unlock the SYSTEM user of SYSTEMDB

178818]{-1}[27/4944874] 2024-10-16 13:19:28.852833 e Authentication catalog_authmgr.cc(00902) : lock time for user SYSTEM is 1440 minutes; user is locked until 2024-10-17 08:14:04.7510000 (given in UTC) [1440,2024-10-17 08:14:04.7510000]

entry.title: Re: #CoE#HHT How to add printer to SAP Cloud Print Manage...
entry.content:
Hello @FFreya  Thanks for your question, It's great to see that you are utilizing SAP Cloud Print Manager. Here are the steps to address your questions:

1. How to add a new local printer to SAP Cloud Print Manager: SAP Cloud Print Manager automatically detects printers that are configured on your local machine. Ensure the printer you want to add is correctly installed and set up on your computer. Here’s how you can typically add a new local printer on your Windows machine, which will then be available in SAP Cloud Print Manager: - Navigate to Settings > Devices > Printers & scanners. - Click "Add a printer or scanner". Windows will search for available printers. - Choose the printer you want to add from the list and follow the on-screen instructions to install it. - Once installed, restart SAP Cloud Print Manager, and the new printer should appear in the list of available printers. If the printer is not detected automatically by SAP Cloud Print Manager after these steps, make sure that the printer is shared in your network (if applicable) and that it’s set as a default printer, as sometimes this can affect visibility.

2. Where to find the printed documents if you choose the printer Microsoft Print to PDF: When you select "Microsoft Print to PDF" as your printer in SAP Cloud Print Manager, it essentially lets you save the output as a PDF file instead of printing it out on paper. Here’s how you can locate or specify where these PDF files are saved: - After selecting "Microsoft Print to PDF" as the printer and initiating the print command, a save dialog box should typically appear asking you where to save the PDF file. - In the dialog box, you can select the desired location (folder/directory) on your computer where you'd like to save the PDF file. You can also rename the file in this step before saving. If, for some reason, you are not seeing this save dialog box, check the printer settings for "Microsoft Print to PDF" in your computer’s printer settings (accessible via **Settings > Devices > Printers & scanners**). Sometimes, there's a default save path set, and you might need to change it to prompt for a location each time.

I hope this resolves your issues. Let me know if you have any more questions!

Best regards

Dave

entry.title: Re: How do we create a variable on WEBI that calculates i...
entry.content:
There not couple function because is not what i wrote. Could you first give us a simplified sample of your dataset ?
ex :

client numbernb dayslab 1lab2
xxx150structure 1structure 2
xxx230structure 2structure 2
xxx30structure 1-
xxx4100structure 1structure 4
...   

Is it something like that ?
So here it will be number([client number]) where ([nbdays]<=60)
or number([client number]) where ([nbdays]<=60 and [lab2]<>"-")

entry.title: Re: Introducing new type of Data Access Control "Operator and Value" for SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Great article. 

Just a quick note. If using the CP or LIKE operator(s) then you need to wrap the value in the first value column in %'s. 
So if I'm looking for say Regions like 'West' my DAC table is looking for:
Criterion: Region

Operator: LIKE

First Value: %West%

There wasn't an example of this up above.

entry.title: Re: Error occured in number assignment for object RESB
entry.content:
Error occured in number assignment for object RESB

Message no. CO168

Diagnosis

An internal error has occurred.

System Response

Processing stopped.

Procedure

Contact your system administrator.

entry.title: SAP Signavio integration using Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Could you please let us know if there are any standard adapters available to integrate SAP Signavio using Cloud Integration(Integration Suite).. or what all is needed from SAP Signavio side like API, or are there any standard APIs available to integrate SAP Signavio ingestions? Thank you!

Ramu

entry.title: Field ISDfCSTEx in ADM1: where is it managed?
entry.content:
Hello guys,
I've had a problem try to saving a "Company details" update having the blocking message below:
"Value not valid; value points to invalid or nonexistent object".

Investigating, I found the field ADM1.ISDfCSTEx, that is related to the ODCI table, which had a value present in the linked table but not compliant to the Country of the company and that causing this saving block.

Please, could someone tell me in which GUI/Interface I should find this field?
I've to understand where the field is managed, but I looked into Company details, general settings and document settings and I didn't find it.

Thank you!!

entry.title: Re: Error in Parser-Thread - CPARSER
entry.content: Well for interest, as you will notice, it is now 2024...13 years after this post started and today I am still got this error and searching brought me here 🙂

entry.title: Re: Can I connect DEV and PRD DMC instances to one PCo?
entry.content:
Hi,

At this point in time, you must consider migrating to SAP Production Connector asap, see https://me.sap.com/notes/3513927 for more details.

Regarding connecting a single SAP Production Connector to multiple SAP Digital Manufacturing instances: No, this is not supported. See: https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-digital-manufacturing/production-connectivity-model/add-production-connector-sap-plant-connectivity-systems, which mentions:

 
Note
A Production Connector can be connected to a single SAP Digital Manufacturing tenant only. Further connections from any other SAP Digital Manufacturing tenant to the same Production Connector are rejected.

Hope it helps,
Manoel

entry.title: Re: why users without access to invoices have access to D...
entry.content:
Hi, @janickovicpeter.

Have you checked the General Authorizations?

Document Draft auth is located within the Sales A/R bracket, even so, it is supposed to serve all marketing documents. So you can set it up like the below and review the other brackets such as Purchasing - A/P.


BR,
Felipe

entry.title: Enabling More Secure and Responsible Generative AI: Key Insights from SAP Teched
entry.content:
The recent SAP TechEd session, presented by @BettinaLaugwitz and Franziska Dobrigkeit, shed light on how SAP is leading the way in ensuring the security and responsibility of generative AI. In an era where AI technologies are becoming omnipresent, SAP is embedding robust ethical guidelines and governance frameworks into its AI processes. 

The Pillars of Responsible AI

Their approach to responsible AI is anchored in three key pillars: AI Safety and Ethics, AI Security, and AI Compliance.

1. AI Safety and Ethics

At the heart of responsible AI lies a commitment to ethical standards and fair outcomes. This involves ensuring that AI systems are designed to promote inclusivity and mitigate biases that can lead to discrimination. As generative AI often learns from uncurated datasets, there’s a pressing need to actively counteract the encoding of harmful patterns within AI models.

SAP’s approach to AI ethics revolves around four key requirements: human agency and oversight, fairness and nondiscrimination, transparency and explainability, and privacy and data governance. The AI Safety and Ethics requirements mandate safeguarding human autonomy through a human-in/on-the-loop approach, ensuring AI systems do not perpetuate marginalization or discrimination, prioritizing transparency and explainability in AI decisions, and protecting privacy while maintaining data integrity. These principles help create AI systems that are trustworthy and equitable.

2. AI Security

As businesses increasingly rely on cloud-based solutions, SAP emphasizes the importance of robust AI security measures. This includes implementing security controls specifically tailored for SAP’s cloud solutions, ensuring that sensitive data remains protected from threats.

Security is paramount in SAP’s AI framework, particularly for cloud solutions. SAP ensures the security of their cloud solutions from SAP and SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) by implementing rigorous AI security controls. These controls protect the data and processes pivotal to AI model training, ensuring that AI solutions are not only effective but also secure from external threats and vulnerabilities.

3. AI Compliance

The evolving landscape of AI regulations, such as the EU AI Act and the US Executive Order 14110, requires organizations to stay ahead of compliance requirements. SAP’s commitment to regulatory adherence ensures that its AI initiatives not only meet legal standards but also align with ethical norms. Compliance with legal and regulatory standards is the third pillar of SAP’s responsible AI strategy. SAP applies stringent checks through audits and regulatory compliance processes to ensure their AI models and data usage meet international standards. They manage data for model training with utmost scrutiny, ensuring compliance with regional and industry-specific regulations.

AI Safety and Ethics

The foundation of SAP’s responsible AI strategy lies in their comprehensive safety and ethical guidelines. SAP’s commitment to AI safety and ethics is divided into internal governance and operationalization on one hand, and adherence to external influences and standards on the other. Internally, the SAP Executive Board, supported by the SAP AI Global Ethics Steering Committee and the AI Ethics Office, drives ethical governance. They rely on guiding principles such as the UNESCO recommendation on the ethics of AI, ensuring these are reflected in product standards and a trustworthy AI workstream.

Externally, SAP aligns with crucial ethical standards and recommendations from organizations like UNESCO, the OECD, IEEE, and more. They adhere to responsible AI regulations like the EU AI Act and the US Executive Order 14110 and take guidance from the SAP AI Global Ethics Advisory Panel. This dual approach ensures that SAP’s AI innovations are ethically sound and globally compliant.

The AI Ethics Assessment Process

SAP’s AI ethics assessment process delineates how AI use cases are evaluated for potential risks and compliance. This process, as depicted in the flowchart image above, involves impact assessments, categorization of cases as redline, high-risk, or standard, and subsequent steps to ensure adherence to SAP's AI ethics policies.

Implementing Responsible AI

SAP has established a comprehensive assessment process to evaluate AI initiatives against key ethical criteria:

  • Human Oversight: Ensuring human agency in AI decision-making processes to maintain autonomy.
  • Fairness and Non-Discrimination: Actively working to prevent biases from being perpetuated in AI outputs.
  • Transparency and Explainability: Striving for clarity in how AI systems are developed and how they operate, even when dealing with complex models.
  • Privacy and Data Governance: Maintaining strict protocols to protect individual privacy and the integrity of data.

Innovative Solutions for Ethical AI

To operationalize these ethical principles, SAP has introduced several innovative solutions:

Orchestration: By designing streamlined AI workflows that connect various components, SAP enables organizations to focus on innovation without getting bogged down in integration challenges. This ensures that AI workflows are powerful yet manageable, connecting diverse components like data pipelines and AI models, as illustrated in the orchestration pipeline flowchart. Orchestration minimizes maintenance while maximizing the efficiency of AI deployment.

  • Grounding: Enhancing the contextual relevance of AI outputs by enriching requests with pertinent business data, reducing the likelihood of inaccurate or misleading responses. This ensures that AI workflows are powerful yet manageable, connecting diverse components like data pipelines and AI models. Orchestration minimizes maintenance while maximizing the efficiency of AI deployment.

  • Data Masking: Protecting sensitive information while retaining the necessary context for AI applications, thereby mitigating biases and safeguarding user privacy. A crucial measure helping to avoid bias and privacy breaches.

  • Content Filtering: Implementing filters to screen out harmful or biased outputs, ensuring that generative AI remains a safe and trustworthy tool for users. This module allows filtering undesired prompts and responses, thereby avoiding biased, harmful, or unethical AI outputs.

As the landscape of AI continues to evolve, fostering responsible innovation will be essential to build trust and ensure a positive impact on society.

SAP’s approach to responsible and secure generative AI, guided by key principles and stringent frameworks, demonstrates a robust model for ethical AI deployment. By combining internal governance with adherence to external standards, SAP not only pioneers in AI innovation but also ensures these advancements are ethical, secure, and compliant. The insights from the SAP TechEd session offer a comprehensive understanding of how SAP addresses the critical challenges of AI ethics and governance, providing a blueprint for the future of responsible AI.

entry.title: Re: Changes In Funds Management with S/4 Public Cloud
entry.content: No, there is no change in OnPremise/Private Cloud.

entry.title: BDC Page scroll Issue
entry.content:
Hello Gurus!!

Greetings !!

Having a problem. Using BDC record i need to complete PGI against Delivery order.

Problem is its skipping one line every time to updated data. I can not find out the problem.Sample code is given here as reference. Please help me to sort it out. Also item number not perfectly maintain which is given in the attachment even its take an extra line also.Its an urgent issue. Please help me.


  LOOP AT it_lips INTO wa_lips.

    indx = indx + 1.

    PERFORM bdc_dynpro      USING 'SAPMV50A' '1000'.

    PERFORM bdc_field       USING 'BDC_OKCODE'
                                  '=POPO_T'.

    PERFORM bdc_dynpro      USING 'SAPMV50A' '0111'.
    PERFORM bdc_field       USING 'BDC_CURSOR'
                                  'RV50A-POSNR'.
    PERFORM bdc_field       USING 'BDC_OKCODE'
                                  '=WEIT'.
    PERFORM bdc_field       USING 'RV50A-POSNR'
                              wa_lips-posnr.


***WHEN ON Below code ONE LINE SKIP  & SCROLL WORK PERFECTLY WITH UPDATED VALUES
****WHEN OFF ITS SCROLL WORKS PERFECTLY BUT DATA NOT UPDATED

    PERFORM bdc_dynpro      USING 'SAPMV50A' '1000'.
    PERFORM bdc_field       USING 'BDC_OKCODE'
                              '/00'.


    DATA(txt_lg) = |LIPS-LGORT({ indx })|.
    PERFORM bdc_field       USING txt_lg
                                  '0004'.

    DATA(txt_dlqt) = |LIPSD-G_LFIMG({ indx })|.
    txt_qt = wa_lips-lfimg.
    PERFORM bdc_field       USING txt_dlqt
                                  txt_qt.


    DATA(txt_pk) = |LIPSD-PIKMG({ indx })|.
    PERFORM bdc_field       USING txt_pk
                                  txt_qt.
  ENDLOOP.


  PERFORM bdc_dynpro      USING 'SAPMV50A' '1000'.
  PERFORM bdc_field       USING 'BDC_OKCODE'
                                '=WABU_T'.
  PERFORM bdc_field       USING 'BDC_CURSOR'
                                'LIPSD-PIKMG(01)'.

  PERFORM bdc_transaction USING 'VL02N' 'A'.

entry.title: Re: It is possible to have a Sandbox Server on SAP RISE for 6-9 months?
entry.content: Hi Paul, any feedback yet or work around? We also need a sandbox server in SAP RISE to test if our industry specific variant configurator is still working and if linked production software running in another cloud subscription is still working fine (no latency issues).

entry.title: Re: SYSTEM user locked
entry.content:
Please look into these KBA from SAP:

  • Reset the SYSTEM User Password in the System Database | SAP Help Portal
  • 2681997 - You have reached maximum number of invalid password entry attempts. User is locked until ... - SAP for Me
  • 2736167 - How to unlock/reactivate the SYSTEM user on SAP HANA? - SAP for Me

entry.title: Re: BDC Page scroll Issue
entry.content: Could you please embed the screenshots instead of hyperlinks? Note that Batch Input (BDC has been obsolete for 20 years) doesn't permit to scroll, instead you must use any function code which permits to reposition (scroll) the table control (it depends on the transaction code and screen). At last resort, you may extend the standard to permit this feature and use it via Batch Input.

entry.title: Content Server 7.53/4 Monitoring in the Solution Manager 7.2
entry.content:
Hello, 

I already know about the following note, describing the approach on how to connect a Content Server for Monitoring:
https://me.sap.com/notes/1895715

it has an attached PDF that acts as Manual. However some informations are missing in my opinion.

If i look at the process for the CS 6.5, it describes how you can attach a Database to the System, however this is not the case with the 7.53/4 CS. It seems that i am not able to add a Database to the System, so that both are linked together. Of couse the Database can be monitored independently, however it is quiet a hassle. As you have no direct link to the System and the Database if you look things up in the LMDB or you have to plan out the System and the database seperatly in the it Calendar. 

I wonder if there is another way of attaching a CS to a Solution Managaer?

Also is it possible to not add them as a system Type "Web Dispatcher" is this is super confusing?

 

Thanks a lot!

entry.title: Service PO price update based on Credit Note
entry.content:
Dear SAP Colleagues,

Please share your experience in handling below scenario in purchasing:

Scenario: Service PO > SES complete & posted > Invoice posted & paid. Now vendor sends a CN, how we can get the PO line item amount updated bringing back the amount equal to CN?

Thanks 

Atif

entry.title: Memeber Management in Dimension Table
entry.content: I have a Dimensions Table for Date in my Model and the Member Management of this Table is set to "User- Managed". How can I reset it ? or set it to "System Managed"?

entry.title: Current phase still on "Prepare in the "Overview" app CALM
entry.content:
Dear all,

In the "Project and Setup" app all the timebox with date has been filled with start and end date. I am currently in the explore phase while prepare is done and overdue. All the type of tasks in the prepare phase (task, requirement,...) are done with status done. Normally they are no open point anymore in the prepare phase, nevertheless when opening the "Overview" app, the dashboard is showing that I am still in Prepare phase while the current phase is past and overdue. You can see below screenshot.

entry.title: Query Regarding API for Retiring Assets to External Customers in SAP Public Cloud 2408
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

I am working on an SAP Public Cloud 2408 project and have encountered a requirement to retire assets in mass to external customers.

Upon investigation, I found that there is an API available in the SAP Public Cloud environment for asset retirement. However, this API seems to support retirement without specifying a customer.

Could you please confirm if there is any API available that supports asset retirement to external customers? If not, kindly advise on alternative solutions or the possibility of an enhancement to meet this requirement.

Looking forward to your prompt assistance.

Best regards,
Syed Ahsan Ali

entry.title: Re: SAP Community - Where are you from?
entry.content:
Welcome everyone!

I am from Egypt living in the Capital Cairo.

entry.title: Block PO creation if not associated to PR
entry.content:
Dear SAP experts, 

I Have a business requirement to block the creation of PO if it's not associated to PR for the document type NB, and for another document type ZNB I can create the PO with no reference to PR 

I tried to use the field selection (ZBNF) and made the field PR mandatory but it's not working *SAP", 

could you please help me, I found some forms talking abour making that mandatory in the user parameter but I did not understand how to that and also my requirement is to make PR manadatory for a certrain docuent type NB, the other doc type ZNB I can create PO with no PR 

 

Thank you so much in advance

Hello,

Regards,

P Ramakrishna

entry.title: Re: Credit Check Rule
entry.content:
Dear Johan,

 

The Check Rules 01, 02 and 03 as defined in the configuration step Define Checking Rules (SSCUI 102623) are SAP standard entries and should not be changed. You can either use these pre-delivered check rules or create your own check rules in customer namespace either Z* or Y* and set them as your default if needed.

Further information regarding the function of Check Rules and the available Check Steps can be found in the SAP Help Portal Credit Check 

 

 

Best Regards,

Priyanka

entry.title: Re: Welcome to the beginner corner!
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano ,

I’m Samia, a recent graduate in economics, passionate about using data to drive business decisions. I’ve developed a keen interest in SAP, particularly in its planning and financial management tools. I’m working toward getting certified through the SAP Digital Skills Initiative to strengthen my expertise in these areas. I’m excited to leverage SAP’s solutions to make meaningful contributions in strategic planning and functional roles in the future.

Looking forward to growing in this field!

entry.title: Re: Why WOCR is not getting selected in WO instead only D...
entry.content: "No activity area was transferred"; Did you sort the Bins? Do they have the right activity area? Please check in /SCWM/LS03 and provide a screenshot.

entry.title: Re: Why WOCR is not getting selected in WO instead only D...
entry.content: The marked line says "The search sequence for activity area is used". There is just space between "area" and "is". Two lines above it says "No activity area was transferred", that is even yellow, that is a warning. So no activity area was used for the determination. Do your bins show the activity area you want for the correct activity? I guess not.

entry.title: Celebrating (almost) 2 Years of the Digital Skills Initiative
entry.content:
Celebrating (almost) 2 Years of the Digital Skills Initiative

In less than two years, the Digital Skills Initiative has made a real impact on people’s lives, helping them gain certification and secure employment within the SAP ecosystem.

October 2024 marks 22 months since the Digital Skills Initiative first launched. Our mission from the outset has been simple: employ new people from underrepresented groups in the SAP ecosystem. Today, we’re proud to celebrate the success and hard work of all participants and partners involved.

The initiative has provided two main avenues for success: a self-study track and instructor-led boot camps. We’ve seen people from all walks of life, including those who may not have had the opportunity otherwise, excel in these programs and go on to achieve certification. Hundreds of participants have not only earned their certifications, but many have also secured jobs (or promotions) with SAP partners or SAP itself, launching promising careers.

Self-Study Success

One of the cornerstones of the initiative has been the self-study track. This flexible option has allowed participants to learn at their own pace, fitting their studies around existing commitments. Many applied and a determined and disciplined few achieved certification and career advancement as a result.

One shining example of a self-study success story is Lin Fan in Germany:

Breaking Barriers and Fighting Sexism: Lin’s Journey from a Salesgirl to an SAP Consultant

Lin has been incredibly supportive of the program following her success and has since started working at SAP. Her decision to start networking and self-studying toward her first certification served as a clear display of motivation for hiring managers.

Boot Camp Triumphs

Our instructor-led boot camps have been intensive, hands-on experiences designed to fast-track participants towards certification. These events provided not only technical training but also mentoring and networking opportunities, bridging the gap between education and employment.

Of course one of our shining bootcamp stars is Chelcie Scott, whose tenacity and persistence were unmatched – leading her to achieve SAP Certification and a start her career in the SAP Ecosystem with one of our partners:

Breaking Down Barriers: Hear from Chelcie Scott How She Broke into Tech with the Help of SAP

Changing Lives, One Certification at a Time

For some, earning an SAP certification has been a life-changing milestone, opening doors to new career opportunities, promotions and stable employment. The feedback from our participants has been overwhelmingly positive and while most of our success stories shall remain anonymous, I’d like to share a few more to drive the point home:

 Learning Her Way Up: The Inspiring Growth Story of Anu in Norway

Beating the Odds: How SAP Skills Shaped Luca’s Career Despite a Struggling Economy

Empowering Dreams Through Digital Skills: A Glimpse Into Karla's Tech Journey with SAP

Areeba's Journey to Learn SAP Skills: Shattering Glass Ceilings in Pakistan

Ashvini's Journey: Reimagining Success through SAP in a New Country

Shoutout to @Anne_Lenhardt  for communicating these stories for us!                                                                                                                                                                                          

The Digital Skills Initiative would not have been possible without the support of our dedicated instructors, SAP Partners, and most importantly, the determination of our participants. Together, we've created a community that supports learning and growth, fostering a more inclusive future within the SAP ecosystem.

As we approach our two-year anniversary, we eagerly look forward to seeing more individuals succeed and transform their lives through this initiative.

For anyone interested in the self-study route of this initiative, please write to us here. Time is of the essence as we will be closing out the self-study program in the next couple of weeks. Details are behind that link. Once you get your free certification exam attempt you will have 12 months to study for and take the exam.

Stay tuned for what’s next!

-David

entry.title: Where can I start learning Billing and Revenue Innovation Management Solution (BRIM)?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Discovering the capabilities of SAP Solutions for Quote-to-Cash Management private cloud (BRIM) for Business User learning journey


Overview
The course describes the processes in SAP CPQ and SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management (BRIM). 
The training gives a process overview about SAP CPQ, SAP Solution Quotation, SAP SOM Subscription Order Management, SAP Convergent Charging, SAP Convergent Mediation, SAP Convergent Invoicing and Financial Contract Accounting. 
Use cases in different industries (High Tech, Postal, Telecommunication) describe how business to customer processes (B2C) and business to business processes (B2B) for subscription and consumption business are implemented with SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management (BRIM).

Learning objectives

After completing this beginner-level learning journey, Business Users will be able to:

  • Understand the Billing and Revenue Innovation Management Solution (BRIM) components.
  • Understand BRIM processes with help of one business example configurable cloud product.
  • Through system demo recordings, Business Users will learn how the BRIM process runs; starting from Solution quotation, rate usage, run dunning process and end with a credit note.
  • Identify Basic Objects in a BRIM Landscape.

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Why WOCR is not getting selected in WO instead only D...
entry.content: Bin Sorting was done as suggested by Laurin Paul, issue resolved , thanks Paul once again

entry.title: Co-ordinate maintenance and new development with gCTS
entry.content:
If you develop your own applications in an ABAP system or need to manage customizing, you might soon reach a point where two types of changes are required at the same time: fix something because an issue has been discovered by your business users and new feature ideas that have to be implemented. The challenge is to be able to continue new development while fixing something else without adding the new features to production when you deliver the fix.
There are processes in the ‘good old’ world of ABAP that have evolved over the years and that are widely used to manage this challenge. And for sure you can continue to use them.
But what if you consider using the git-enabled CTS (gCTS)? How do processes change? What are the tools that you can use?
To say that from the beginning: with gCTS, you can handle your own development objects and customizing. For maintenance in the sense of implementing service packs provided by SAP, SAP Notes. Or for managing modifications that you might have done to SAP-owned objects, you need to use CTS when it comes to transports.
And in addition: in most of the cases, you would still need the same number of systems: separate development systems for feature development and maintenance and maybe also separated test systems. This is because the basic paradigm of ABAP that there can only be one active version of an object in a system at a time is not changed with gCTS. gCTS is another way of storing versions and managing what is deployed or imported where but the basics of ABAP are not subject to the competences of gCTS.
So, what can you do?
In principle, gCTS offers the option to work with a single system landscape consisting of a development, a test and a production system. Different versions of objects can be reflected by the help of different branches. The branches of your repository could then for example look like in the following figure:

Production would be the active branch on the production system – that’s easy 😉
For development and test systems, you would need to switch branches. Maybe the standard would be that the development and test system have the feature branch as active branch so that new functionality can be created.
In case an issue comes up in production, development and test need to be switched to a maintenance branch. This branch should be created out of the current state of the production or main branch. This branch stays active until fixes are done. Then the branch with the fix needs to be merged into development, main and production. After all tests are finished and the fix has arrived in production, the branch containing the fix can be deleted and the development branch can again become active in the development and test system.
Looks like a great approach to reduce the number of systems? It is, when you just look at the systems. But you need to consider our workforce… while a fix is done, no new development is possible. So, if you do not develop a lot and only have very few developers, this might be an option. But if more people – and in this case already two to three might be considered as ‘more – this approach comes with challenges and a lot of dele time. You have to carefully coordinate when you do what: switching a branch means that the coding of that branch is to become active. Changes to the same objects that are not released (and therewith committed to the repository) will be overwritten, which means that they are lost after the branch switch. Developers working on new features cannot continue their work while a maintenance branch is active and have to wait until all activities around fixing the big are finished.
So, using this approach to reduce the number of systems requires good planning and organizational measures to make sure that no work gets lost, and it can result in times where development is not possible for some of your team members.
If reducing the number of systems is not your main goal, you can nevertheless profit from using gCTS to co-ordinate maintenance and development within your system landscape. You would need two development systems and most probably two test systems, as well. One development and one test system are used for maintenance and the respective other ones for new development. Only the systems that you currently need have to be up and running. The branch design of the repository is the same as described above. The maintenance systems are connected to the production branch as long as no fix is done and are by default shut down. When a fix is needed, the first step (after having started the systems for sure) would be to create a new branch out of the production branch for the required fix and make this branch the active one in the maintenance development and test system. Only after that, you should open up the system for developers to do the fix. When the fix is successfully tested, the branch needs to get merged into all other branches including the development branch. Now, maintenance systems need to get closed for development. The fix can be deployed to production, the production branch becomes again the active branch in the maintenance systems and the branch that was used for fixing should be deleted. With this, you can make sure that you get the current production state of your software is available in the maintenance systems whenever a fix has to be done.
On the system for new development, you only need a short pause in development when a fix has been merged into the development branch to pull the changes and at some point in the merge process, you also have to solve conflicts in case an object is concerned by the fix that you also changed for new features. gCTS supports you in detecting the conflicts and solving them.

entry.title: SAP Service and Asset Manager 2405 Sync issues
entry.content:
We are very frequently getting -  HTTP Code 401 with message: <html><meta http-equiv="refresh" content="0"><body onload="Javascript:document.location.reload(tru e);"><H1>Your browser is not configured for using SPNego<) during sync. 

But after synchronising for couple of times, this error goes away and the sync is successful.

When we get above error, in the gateway traces we do not see any requests coming in, so we are currently under the impression that this issue must be because of either the web dispatcher or the cloud connector.

We have checked the logs but unable to find any leads on this issue. Any inputs please on this issue?

# SAP Service and Asset Manager

entry.title: Re: BDC Page scroll Issue
entry.content:
Hello @hossain_rajib55 

Why don't you use WS_DELIVERY_UPDATE function or Delivery Interface to update picking quantities and post GI? It's documented, supported, much easier than error prone BDC.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: SD/EWM Real Open Quantities in Sales Schedule Agreeme...
Re: SD/EWM Real Open Quantities in Sales Schedule Agreement / Schedule Lines
entry.content:
Hi RAF, Thanks for the quick response, I forgot to mention we are using sales schedule agreement + schedule lines (forecast delivery schedule) not sales orders, here is our configuration right now: 

Av. CheckDescriptionRule Availability CheckAvail. Check Rule
SPStock and Plan. Rec.Sales OrderDeliverySD Order MTSSD Order ConsDialog Box (Batch/BAPI Delivery Proposal)
SRStock and Rel. Rec.Sales OrderDeliverySD Order MTSSD Order ConsDialog Box (Batch/BAPI Delivery Proposal)
STConsider Only StockSales OrderDeliverySD Order MTSSD Order ConsDialog Box (Batch/BAPI Delivery Proposal)
ZPStock & Plan Rec KCSales OrderDeliverySD Order MTSSD Order ConsDialog Box (Batch/BAPI Delivery Proposal)
ZRStock & Rel Rec KCSales OrderDeliverySD Order MTSSD Order ConsDialog Box (Batch/BAPI Delivery Proposal)
ZTConsid Only Stock KCSales OrderDeliverySD Order MTSSD Order ConsDialog Box (Batch/BAPI Delivery Proposal)

Sales Order Items - Backorders looks very good, but unfortunately only shows Sales Orders and cannot be filtered by another SD document category such as Sales Schedule Agreement. Any suggestions?

entry.title: Similar use of FM SAVE_TEXT, READ_TEXT in S4 Hana cloud for Standard Text
entry.content:
We want to use the data given in the standard text app Manage Standard Text in our RAP Class Eclipse ADT.

  1. How to read the data given in the standard text in RAP Class Eclipse ADT?
  2. How to save additional data in the given standard text through RAP Class in Eclipse ADT?

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Similar use of FM SAVE_TEXT, READ_TEXT in S4 Hana clo...
entry.content:
1. In S/4HANA CLoud the CDS view I_OperationStandardText for reading Standard Text  ( https://api.sap.com/cdsviews/I_OPERATIONSTANDARDTEXT )

2. Unfortunately there is no API similar to SAVE_TEXT to update Standard operation Text . Please submit a feature request if you would like it added

entry.title: To print the attached PDF files to a maintenance operation in a new SAP Form Template.
entry.content:
Is it possible to print all the PDF attachment for a maintenance operation in the new form which is being developed similar to Operation Control Ticket? What challenges are there to develop this functionality? 

Can someone tell us the steps or the feasibility for this? Which all configuration needs to be maintained to have or use the new form to appear in the work order print function?

entry.title: Re: Sales order specific PO to vendor based on intercompa...
entry.content: Don't worry, just mark as "Accepted Solution" your own answer 😉

entry.title: 60% OFF Pass [SAP C_TFG51_2405] Exams using (QA4exam.com) PDF & Online Practice Test Questions
entry.content: QA4Exam customizable practice tests emulate the actual exam environment, allowing you to become familiar with the format and time constraints. This simulation is essential in boosting your confidence for the real exam day.

entry.title: Re: Welcome to the beginner corner!
entry.content: I realize the importance of continuous learning and growth in an ever-evolving tech landscape. Pursuing this certification is not just about adding another credential—it's about deepening my expertise, validating the skills I've gained, and positioning myself to better solve complex challenges in my field. By staying current and certified, I can contribute more effectively to my team's success and drive innovation with confidence. This certification aligns with my long-term career goals and the demands of the industry, making it a vital step forward @David_Chaviano

entry.title: Re: BDC Page scroll Issue
entry.content:
@Sandra_Rossi  Please check now. Scroll is working but it skip one line . In internal table i got all the values without interval.

@DominikTylczyn  I create a BAPI for doing it automatically using API.

entry.title: MIR4 - Add custom fields in vendor data subscreen 6510 in program SAPLMR1M
entry.content:
Hello guys,

I'm having the request to add custom fields in program SAPLMR1M subscreen 6510 from MIR4 / MIRO transaction.

I already checked tons of posts but I didn't find a clear solution without adapt standard code.

I found some tutorials to add custom tab in MIRO or add custom fields in the header but nothing concerning this specific screen 6510 that concerns the vendor information.

I already checked BADI's MRM_ITEM_CUSTFIELDS or BADI_FDCB_SUBBAS* and also all the user-exist or MIRO transaction

Exit Name Description

LMR1M001 Exit utilisateur dans le contrôle des factures de logistique
LMR1M002 Regroupement des comptes pour détermination comptes EM/EF
LMR1M003 Attribution numéros ds contrôle des factures de logistique
LMR1M004 Contrôle factures logist. : textes postes pour doc. suivants
LMR1M005 Ctrle factures logist. : validation pr enreg. doc. préenreg.
MRMH0001 Contrôle factures logistique : facturation automatique
MRMH0002 Contrôle des factures logistique : message EDI entrant
MRMH0003 Contrôle des factures logistique : revalorisation/RAP
MRMN0001 Edition/Création de messages - contrôle factures logistique
LMR1M006 Contrôle des factures logistique : traiter facture XML

Can you please tell me if anyone already had this kind of request ?
Thank you for your help !

https://www.sapyard.com/steps-to-add-custom-tab-in-standard-miro-item-screen-using-badi-mrm_item_custfields/

https://sapyard.com/add-custom-fields-in-standard-miro-header-screen-through-badi_fdcb_subbas01/

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Vpe511bIsE8

https://abapers.wordpress.com/tag/olmrlist/

entry.title: Re: Integrate Sharepoint online with Datasphere
entry.content: Thanks Koray. It helped me set up connectivity for files.
